pitch.subject: 3 ICT-focused topic ideas for Forex Crunch readers
pitch.body:
Hi there,

I noticed Forex Crunch does a solid job covering platform comparisons and broker reviews for traders just getting started — but there's a gap I keep seeing on most forex blogs: almost nothing on how ICT concepts actually apply once someone is trading a funded account.

I'm Harvest Wright, founder of R2F Trading and an ICT coach who has spent 10+ years helping prop firm traders pass funded challenges on platforms like FTMO and MyForexFunds. My students are actively getting funded, and I publish in-depth content on the mechanics behind why — order blocks, fair value gaps, liquidity sweeps, and session-based risk management.

I think your audience would find real value in a few angles I haven't seen covered this way elsewhere:

- How to Use ICT Order Blocks to Pass Your FTMO Challenge Without Blowing a Single Rule
- The 1-Hour Killzone Mistake That Kills 9 Out of 10 Funded Challenges
- Fair Value Gaps vs. Traditional Support and Resistance: What Forex Crunch Readers Need to Know in 2024

For reference, I've published 40+ long-form trading articles, have a growing YouTube channel at @R2F-Trading, and was featured as a TradingView Editors' Pick — so I'm used to writing for an audience that takes this seriously.

Open to any of these? Happy to send a full outline whichever direction looks like the best fit.

Harvest Wright
Founder, R2F Trading
r2ftrading.com

topics.title: How to Use ICT Order Blocks to Pass Your FTMO Challenge Without Blowing a Single Rule
topics.angle: Most prop firm failures aren't about strategy — they're about misreading institutional order flow, and this piece shows forex traders exactly how to fix that using ICT order block logic.

topics.title: The 1-Hour Killzone Mistake That Kills 9 Out of 10 Funded Challenges
topics.angle: New and intermediate forex traders consistently overtrade outside high-probability ICT killzones, and this piece gives them a concrete session-based framework to stop doing it.

topics.title: Fair Value Gaps vs. Traditional Support and Resistance: What Forex Crunch Readers Need to Know in 2024
topics.angle: As ICT concepts go mainstream, forex traders using legacy S/R methods are leaving clean entries on the table — this piece bridges the gap for readers already familiar with platform-level charting tools.
